checks.finding: Core/evidence tool names, resource URIs, error codes, and root-resolution priority (CLI arg > SDD_FORGE_ROOT > cwd, REQ-007) are enumerated concretely; two implementers would converge on the same behavior.

Round-2 size-limit conflict is resolved: requirements.md Edge Cases now fixes the threshold at 2 MiB and names the error code `too-large` (line 152-153), matching acceptance-tests.md AC-017 (line 21) exactly. No other conflict found between REQ-006 denylist scope and the Non-goals exclusion of signature verification, nor between REQ-007 root-fixing and REQ-016/AC-016 root-immutability.

Edge Cases section (lines 148-157) enumerates missing tasks.md, malformed tasks.md, missing AGENTS.md section, oversized/binary file, non-SDD root, and Windows path handling, each traced to a named error code and an AC (AC-002, AC-003/AC-004, AC-012, AC-017).

Assumptions section states Node>=20 presence, target-repo SDD structure, stdio-only scope, and check-task-state.sh as the equivalence source of truth; the one open item (OQ-R1, MCP list naming) is explicitly marked non-blocking with an owner and a resolution checkpoint (tasks.md approval).

This feature is a read-only status server; Non-goals explicitly exclude Approval-state changes and write tools, so no human-approval or irreversible-change workflow boundary is introduced by this spec.
Tool/resource inventories, error-code taxonomy, size threshold, and root-resolution precedence are specific enough that a design reviewer would not need to invent missing product behavior to proceed.
